opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My entire body feels like it was crushed in a vice, and I jump at the slightest sound.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on over the last half day and I can't get out of bed — I'm desperate for something to stop the pain.

symptom_narrative.onset: it built up over the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: about half a day and getting worse
symptom_narrative.character: a crushing, burning pain all over, and my muscles feel locked up
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my whole body, but my back muscles feel like they're in spasm
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: a steady climb from a mild ache to this
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: almost nothing — lying dead still and darkness help a tiny bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any noise or light, and trying to move or bend
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I'm on edge, jumping at every sound, and I feel panicky
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had flares since my car accident two years ago but never this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't get from lying down to standing on my own

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: chronic widespread pain since a car accident two years ago

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: I took my whole day's worth in about four hours today and it did nothing

family_history_as_reported.narrative: nothing relevant that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live alone and I'm frightened and desperate for relief

communication_style.typical_message_style: urgent, frightened messages emphasising unbearable pain and asking for strong medication

objective_data_offered.value: my back muscles feel rock hard and in spasm
my urine looked normal colour when I last went
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-report; not a laboratory assessment for myoglobinuria
